records.ety: [OE. exponen, expounen, expounden, fr. L. exponere to set out, expose, expound; ex out + ponere to put: cf. OE. expondre, expondre. See Position.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Expounded; p. pr. & vb. n. Expounding.]

defs: To lay open; to expose to view; to examine.
To lay open the meaning of; to explain; to clear of obscurity; to interpret; as, to expound a text of Scripture, a law, a word, a meaning, or a riddle.
quotes: Expound this matter more fully to me.
